gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/45)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/45)
text: إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ كَنُجُومِ السَّمَاءِ كُلَّمَا غَابَ نَجْمٌ طَلَعَ نَجْمٌ حَتَّى إِذَا أَشَرْتُمْ بِأَصَابِعِكُمْ وَ مِلْتُمْ بِأَعْنَاقِكُمْ غَيَّبَ اللَّهُ عَنْكُمْ نَجْمَكُمْ فَاسْتَوَتْ بَنُو عَبْدِ الْمُطَّلِبِ فَلَمْ يُعْرَفْ أَيٌّ مِنْ أَيٍّ فَإِذَا طَلَعَ نَجْمُكُمْ فَاحْمَدُوا رَبَّكُمْ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hanan Bin Sadeyr, from Marouf Bin Mharrabouz,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘But rather, we<sup>asws</sup> are like the stars of the sky. Every time one star disappears, one emerges, to the extent when you indicate with your fingers and tilt with your necks. Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Hide your star from you all. So the Clan of Abdul Muttalib<sup>as</sup> would be evenly established and it would not be recognised which one is from which. So when your star does emerge, then Praise your Lord<sup>azwj</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 8. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hannan ibn Sadir from Ma‘ruf ibn Kharrabudh from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following. “We are only like the stars in the heavens. Whenever one star disappears (from sight) another one comes in view until you will point out with your fingers and make a gesture with your necks. Allah will cause your star to disappear from your sight. The descendants of ‘Abd al-Muttalib will all look similar as such that one would not know which is whiCh When your star will reappear then you must give thanks to Allah.”
